rulings.Case Title: DELINDA MACUT VS. GRUBHUB HOLDINGS INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2024, Line 1. DEFENDANTS GRUBHUB HOLDINGS, INC., AND GRUBHUB INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Defendant's renewed motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: YADIRA GUTIERREZ VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2024, Line 7. DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar. Plaintiff's request to stay this case pending the California Supreme Court's resolution of Rodriguez v. FCA US, LLC (2022) 77 Cal.App.5th 209 is granted. (CCP 128(a)(8).) The main issue in this case is whether the express warranty provisions of the Song-Beverly Act apply to used vehicles. There is a split of authority regarding that issue and the California Supreme Court recently heard argument on the matter. This court anticipates that the split between Rodriguez and Stiles v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2024) 101 Cal.App.5th 913, which is also under review, will be resolved shortly. Defendant may seek to lift the stay and calendar an updated summary judgment motion after the California Supreme Court issues its decision. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party (plaintiff) is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: J. DENNIS BONNEY VS. 1810 JACKSON STREET, A CONDOMINIUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2024, Line 9. DEFENDANT 1810 JACKSON STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants' "motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiff's complaint" is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 435.5. Counsel says a telephonic meet-and-confer occurred September 10. (Gopal Dec. 3:8-10.) However, an amendment to the first amended complaint was filed September 30 and CCP 435.5(a) provides: "If an amended pleading is filed," the parties "shall meet and confer again" The court finds no reference to such a second meet-and-confer in the register of actions. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: 123 CORBETT, LP VS. TALBCO DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Karin N. Kuemerle
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2024, Line 10. DEFENDANT TALBCO DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint. Defendant Talbco Development Corp.'s motion for leave to file cross-complaint is denied. In this construction defect case, Talbco was general contractor for renovations of plaintiff's building. Talbco had at least eight subcontractors. In 2021, plaintiff sent defect reports to Talbco - reports that no doubt alerted Talbco to claims against its subcontractors. (Opp. 1:12-19.) In its December 29, 2023 answer, Talbco asserted that other parties (e.g., its subcontractors) were responsible for damage. (Id. at 2:3-10.) On January 8, plaintiff's counsel urged Talbco to cross-claim against the subcontractors immediately so trial would not be delayed. (Maltzer Dec. 1:4-11.) However, Talbco waited until now - four months before the February 24 trial date - to seek leave to cross-claim against the eight subcontractors. Were leave granted, discovery would likely push trial deep into 2025 or 2026. Liberal amendment of pleadings is common in California, but a court may exercise its discretion to deny where the moving party has been dilatory. (Crocker Nat. Bank v. Emerald (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 852, 864.) This is such a case. Talbco's memorandum offers no explanation for its foot-dragging. Moreover, Talbco concedes it can bring an indemnity action against the subcontractors should plaintiff defeat it at trial. (Memo. 5:16-17.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: PETER RIVELIS VS. ENLIGHTENED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2024, Line 12. DEFENDANT ENLIGHTENED MANAGEMENT COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Enlightened Entertainment Co.'s "motion to strike portions of plaintiff's complaint" is denied. Punitive damages are adequately alleged for the pleading stage. For example, EMC failed to pay plaintiff his lawful wages, so he complained of the illegality to the California Labor Commission. (Cmplt. 3:3-23.) Plaintiff then settled with EMC for half of what he was owed only to be fired the next day without cause. (Id.) Joint employer is also adequately alleged under California's liberal pleading standards. The sole contrary opinion EMC's memorandum cites is from a federal court that does not follow California procedural law. (Memo. 7:14-17.) Interest is an issue typically decided at trial after all facts are in. Medical expenses are not limited to workers' compensation. The motion to strike is not "a procedural 'line item veto' for the civil defendant." (PH II, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KANE LAPLANTE VS. UCSF MEDICAL CENTER, A BUSINESS ENTITY FORM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2024, Line 14. 1 - DEFENDANT SURESH GUNASEKARAN, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, NADER NAJAFI, MEGAN BETANCOURT, TYRONE JOHNSON's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to count 6 of the complaint is overruled. The count specifically pleads the elements of abuse of a dependent adult under a neglect theory. (CACI 3103; Cmplt. 19:24-24:20.) Deliberate disregard is not an element of this cause of action, as defendants assert. (Dmr. 2:6-11; CACI 3103.) Whether the complaint adequately pleads enhanced remedies (CACI 3104) is not directly addressed by the demurrer; in any event, on general demurrer the test is whether the complaint states any valid claim and this one does. (See Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38-39.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KANE LAPLANTE VS. UCSF MEDICAL CENTER, A BUSINESS ENTITY FORM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2024, Line 14. 2 - DEFENDANT SURESH GUNASEKARAN, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, NADER NAJAFI, MEGAN BETANCOURT, AND TYRONE JOHNSON's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants' motion to strike parts of the complaint has two grounds. First, defendants say the "claims for Elder Abuse [sic] are subject to demurrer," so enhanced remedies are "non-recoverable." (Mot. 2:13-15.) But the demurrer has been overruled, so this part of the motion is denied. Second, defendants rightly note that the complaint's prayer for $1.95 million in non-economic damages (id. at 25:11) runs afoul of Civil Code 3333.2(f), so this part of the motion is granted. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BRODY KYLER VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 24, 2024, Line 15. DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, AND RASIER-CA, LLC's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Defendants' (UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, RASIER-CA, LLC) motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
